evidences.title: Binance Historical Tick Data (1 second) for a specific date ...
evidences.content: Optimus Prime's user avatar ## 1 Answer 1 Binance provides some of its historical trading data on the `binance.vision` domain. Specifically, the 1 second klines for BTCUSDT pair can be found here: `binance.vision` Here's a sample bash script that downloads and unpacks the raw data for the whole month: `#!/bin/bash for i in $(seq -f "%02g" 1 31) # zero-padded days 01, 02, 03, ... 31 do # month of july 2023, day number is the `i` variable archiveName="BTCUSDT-1s-2023-07-${i}.zip" # download the .zip, extract it (saves .csv), and remove the .zip wget " && unzip "./${archiveName}" && rm "./${archiveName}" done` As mentioned in their GitHub readme, each of the the `.csv` lines contains the following field values: `.csv` [...] `.csv` | Open time | Open | High | Low | Close | Volume | Close time | Quote asset volume | Number of trades | Taker buy base asset volume | Taker buy quote asset volume | Ignore | --- --- --- --- --- --- | So for example, the file `BTCUSDT-1s-2023-07-01.csv` containing this first line `BTCUSDT-1s-2023-07-01.csv` `1688169600000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,0.54114000,1688169600999,16489.61790060,31,0.52320000,15942.95040000,0` enab...

evidences.title: How to Download Historical Crypto Data from Binance?
evidences.content: The script will: Find all active USDT perpetual futures on Binance Download daily candle data for each one Save separate CSV files for each symbol Show you progress in real-time Give you a summary of what succeeded or failed ## What the Data Looks Like Each CSV contains: timestamp: Exact time of each daily candle open, high, low, close: Price data volume: Trading volume date: trade date symbol: Which contract it is (e.g., BTC/USDT:USDT) Clean, organized, ready for Excel, Python, or any analysis tool. The script respects Binance’s API limits so you don’t get blocked. If one symbol fails, it keeps going and reports which ones had issues. [...] markets = self.exchange.load_markets() perp_futures = [] for symbol, market in markets.items(): if (market[’type’] == ‘swap’ and market[’linear’] and market[’active’] and symbol.endswith(’USDT’)): perp_futures.append(symbol) print(f”Found {len(perp_futures)} USDT perpetual futures”) return perp_futures def download_daily_data(self, symbol, start_date=None, days_back=365): “”“Download daily OHLC for a symbol”“” try: if start_date: since = int(datetime.strptime(start_date, ‘%Y-%m-%d’).timestamp() 1000) else: since = int((datetime.now() - timed...

evidences.title: Save Historical data from Binance
evidences.content: ``` frombinance.client import Client # fetch 1 minute klines for the last day up until now klines = get_historical_klines("BNBBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE, "1 day ago UTC") # fetch 30 minute klines for the last month of 2017 klines = get_historical_klines("ETHBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_30MINUTE,"1 Dec, 2017","1 Jan, 2018") # fetch weekly klines since it listed klines = get_historical_klines("NEOBTC", KLINE_INTERVAL_1WEEK,"1 Jan, 2017") ``` The full code can be found in the examples folder of the python-binance package. ### Save to file Once we have fetched the list of klines, it makes sense to save them to a file for later use. [...] `get_historical_klines` has been added to the binance.client, so now all you need to do is run ``` import json frombinance.client import Client client = Client("", "") klines = client. get_historical_klines("ETHBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_30MINUTE,"1 Dec, 2017","1 Jan, 2018") ``` ### Next Steps With these cached klines we can open them at a later date and run backtesting on them. See the related [Kucoin post][kucoin-post] to download historical klines on Kucoin. Subsequent posts will look at how to use pandas and TA-Lib for some simple ba...

evidences.content: Return type: : DataFrame") binance\_history.fetch\_data(symbol, asset\_type, data\_type, start, end, tz=None, timeframe=None)# : Parameters: : symbol (str")) – The binance market pair name. e.g. `'BTCUSDT'`. start (datetime")) – The start datetime of requested data. If it’s an instance of `datetime.datetime`, asset\_type (str")) – The asset type of requested data. It must be one of `'spot'`, `'futures/um'`, `'futures/cm'`. data\_type (str")) – The type of requested data. It must be one of `'klines'`, `'agg_trades'`. it’s timezone is ignored. If it’s a `str`, it should be parsed by dateutil, e.g. `"2022-1-18:10"`. end (datetime")) – [...] Returns: : A pandas dataframe with columns open, high, low, close, volume, trades, close\_datetime. the dataframe’s index is the open datetime of klines, the timezone of the datetime is set by `tz`, if it is None, your local timezone will be used. Return type: : DataFrame") [...] binance-history 0.1.7 documentation Back to top Edit this page # Welcome to binance-history’s documentation# binance-history is a Python library for fetching cryptocurrency klines or trades data. evidences.title: Binance Historical 2017-2025 (all USDT pairs)
evidences.content: ## About Dataset This dataset contains historical cryptocurrency market data extracted from public Binance API endpoints. The files provided include 1-minute (1m) candlestick (kline) data for all USDT trading pairs, downloaded and updated programmatically from 2017 to 2024. Data is stored in efficient Parquet format for compatibility and performance, covering open, high, low, close, and volume for each time interval. This dataset is ideal for quantitative research, backtesting trading strategies, machine learning projects, and market analysis. Source: (public API, no account required) Updated: Regularly (monthly and daily backfill). Columns: timestamp, open, high, low, close, volume, symbol, and more. Coverage: Global cryptocurrency markets. ## Usability info 5.88 [...] arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2017 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2018 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2019 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2020 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2021 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2022 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2023 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USDT_2024 arrow_right folder klines_1m_TRADING_USD...

evidences.content: Pro tip: You can also filter by exchange, e.g. `?filter_exchange_id=COINBASE` or `?filter_exchange_id=BINANCE` ### 1.2: Check Data Coverage (How Far Back It Goes) Still using the `BINANCE_SPOT_BTC_USDT` example, look for: "data\_trade\_start": "2017-07-14T00:00:00Z", "data\_orderbook\_start": "2017-12-18T00:00:00Z" This tells you: Trades available since: July 14, 2017 Order books available since: December 18, 2017 🎯 These fields are available for every supported symbol, not just BTC/USDT. You can repeat this check for ETH/USD on Coinbase, DOGE/USDT on OKX, etc. ## Step 2: Should You Use REST or Flat Files? Once you’ve confirmed the data exists, choose how to access it. [...] | | | | --- | Use Case | Use REST API | Use Flat Files | | Quick data access (1–30 days) | ✅ | ❌ | | Snapshots of order books or OHLCV | ✅ | ❌ | | Full trade history over months/years | ⚠️ (slow) | ✅ | | Tick-by-tick order book updates | ❌ | ✅ | | Massive backtests, full dataset exports | ❌ | ✅ | | Avoid rate limits & pagination | ❌ | ✅ | ### Real Example from a University Researcher “I need historical order book data (going back a few years) for BTC/USDT. evidences.title: Introducing historical market data from Binance.US
evidences.content: We’re excited to offer free access to historical market data from the Binance.US exchange. At launch, our datasets include historical candlesticks, trades, and aggregated trade data for all trading pairs. While these datasets are available to all Binance.US customers, API traders in particular may find historical market data useful for backtesting and analysis. Using historical market data Historical market data from Binance.US includes price, order quantity, and trading volume data, providing traders and analysts with valuable insights into historical trends. Our historical data library supports market data dating back to September 2019. Going forward, we will continually update our datasets at daily and monthly intervals. Download historical market data for free [...] Download historical market data for free To get started, visit the Historical Market Data page to browse available data products. You can download our free datasets as a .zip file, or follow the instructions on the page to download programmatically. Download the Binance.US app to trade on the go: iOS | Android Follow us to stay up to date with Binance.US news and announcements: logo-black.png image2.png image1.pn...
